title: richetia iliasii
intro: Richetia iliasii, also known as the African Stinging Tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia, characterized by its stinging hairs that contain a toxin causing intense pain and inflammation. Richetia iliasii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. Richetia iliasii is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or topical applications, and its use should be approached with caution due to its potent and potentially hazardous properties.
